decks.notes: The deck focuses on quickly filling the graveyard with creatures to then reanimate them using spells like Unearth and Persist. It aims to overwhelm the opponent with powerful creatures like Archon of Cruelty and Emperor of Bones, while using cards like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push to control the board. The combo potential of the deck lies in bringing back key creatures from the graveyard to create a powerful board presence, while also disrupting the opponent's strategy with hand disruption and removal spells.

This deck aims to put pressure on the opponent by utilizing aggressive creatures like Quantum Riddler, Ragavan, and Phelia while disrupting their plans with cards like Solitude and Prismatic Ending. Consign to Memory and Fable of the Mirror-Breaker provide additional ways to deal with threats and generate card advantage. The deck also includes control elements like Spell Snare and Ephemerate to maintain board presence and protect its creatures. With a mix of efficient threats and disruptive spells, this deck looks to overwhelm opponents quickly and efficiently.

The main focus of this Boros Aggro deck is to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of quick creature spells like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Guide of Souls, along with burn spells like Galvanic Discharge and Goblin Bombardment. The deck also incorporates strong removal spells like Thraben Charm and Ranger-Captain of Eos to maintain board control. With cards like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Arena of Glory providing extra aggression and resilience, the deck aims to secure a fast and efficient victory through a combination of creature threats and burn damage.

The deck aims to control the game by disrupting opponents' strategies and out-valuing them with efficient removal spells and counterspells. It uses cards like Fatal Push and Counterspell to deal with threats efficiently while also generating card advantage with cards like Consult the Star Charts and Wan Shi Tong, Librarian. The deck also utilizes cards like Field of Ruin and Sheoldred's Edict to disrupt opponents' mana bases and creature strategies. Ultimately, the goal is to outlast opponents and win the game through attrition and card advantage.

The deck is focused on aggressive gameplay and maximizing damage output. It utilizes a mix of powerful creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Territorial Kavu, along with burn spells such as Lightning Bolt to quickly reduce the opponent's life total. Leyline of the Guildpact and Leyline Binding help to control the board, while cards like Scion of Draco and Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury provide additional threats. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of creatures and spells, while also having some disruption tools like Stubborn Denial and Consign to Memory.

The deck focuses on quickly filling the graveyard with creatures, using cards like Street Wraith and Generous Ent, to then cast Living End and bring all those creatures back onto the battlefield. Leyline of the Guildpact and Leyline Binding help protect the combo from disruption, while Shardless Agent and Endurance provide additional support and control options. The deck aims to overwhelm opponents with a sudden surge of powerful creatures and maintain board control through various means like subtlety and force of negation. The key strategy is to efficiently execute the combo and outvalue opponents in the mid to late game.

This deck focuses on quickly generating mana through spells like Pyretic Ritual and Manamorphose, while using Ral, Monsoon Mage and Ruby Medallion to reduce the cost of spells. By casting a series of spells, the deck aims to build up to a lethal Grapeshot or combo off with Past in Flames to recur spells from the graveyard. The deck also utilizes Wish to search for key pieces from the sideboard, providing flexibility in different matchups. Valakut Awakening and Reckless Impulse help dig for combo pieces, while Wrenn's Resolve protects the combo from disruption.

The deck focuses on using the UrzaTron lands (Urza's Mine, Power Plant, and Tower) to quickly ramp up to play powerful colorless Eldrazi creatures and planeswalkers. It aims to control the game by using cards like All Is Dust, Dismember, and Chalice of the Void to disrupt the opponent's strategies. Karn, the Great Creator is a key card that can shut down artifacts and provide additional utility. The deck also utilizes cards like Trinisphere and Ugin, the Spirit Dragon to maintain board control and overwhelm the opponent with powerful threats.

This UW Control deck focuses on controlling the board with removal spells and counterspells while using cards like Narset, Parter of Veils and Orim's Chant to disrupt the opponent's strategy. The deck also includes powerful planeswalkers like Teferi, Time Raveler to provide card advantage and slow down aggressive strategies. The use of Isochron Scepter allows for repeated use of key instant spells like Counterspell or Prismatic Ending. The deck aims to outvalue the opponent and grind out a win through incremental advantage and powerful late-game threats like Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury.

This deck focuses on aggressive play with a mix of disruptive elements to control the opponent's game plan. It combines efficient creatures like Phelia and Ragavan with removal spells like Galvanic Discharge and Prismatic Ending to keep the opponent on their back foot. Teferi, Time Raveler and Quantum Riddler provide additional disruption and card advantage, while cards like Fable of the Mirror-Breaker and Wrath of the Skies offer powerful finishers. The deck aims to pressure the opponent early and maintain control of the game while closing out quickly.

The deck focuses on using Allosaurus Rider along with Eldritch Evolution and Neoform to quickly search for and put powerful creatures into play, such as Griselbrand and Ghalta, Stampede Tyrant. The deck also includes cards like Pact of Negation and Veil of Summer to protect the combo pieces and disrupt the opponent's strategies. With ramp cards like Uro, the Song Unending and Bridgeworks Battle, the deck aims to set up explosive turns and win the game through a fast and efficient combo.

The deck focuses on utilizing creatures with various abilities to generate value and combo off using cards like Agatha's Soul Cauldron, Yawgmoth, Thran Physician, and Walking Ballista. It includes toolbox creatures like Strangleroot Geist and Endurance to deal with different types of threats. The deck also features tutoring cards like Green Sun's Zenith to find the necessary pieces for the combo. Overall, the strategy revolves around efficiently using creatures and spells to create a strong board presence and overwhelm the opponent with powerful combos.

The main idea of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of early aggressive creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Territorial Kavu, backed up by powerful enchantments like Leyline of the Guildpact and Leyline Binding to control the board. The deck also utilizes efficient removal spells like Lightning Bolt and Stubborn Denial to disrupt the opponent's game plan. Additionally, the inclusion of cards like Scion of Draco and Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury provides a powerful late game threat. The mana base is optimized for consistency and flexibility in casting spells of various colors.

The deck focuses on assembling the UrzaTron lands (Urza's Tower, Urza's Mine, Urza's Power Plant) to generate a large amount of mana quickly. It uses powerful colorless Eldrazi creatures and planeswalkers like Karn and Ugin to control the board and disrupt the opponent's strategy. By ramping up mana with cards like Mind Stone and Expedition Map, the deck can quickly deploy threats and overwhelm the opponent. It also utilizes disruptive cards like Thought-Knot Seer and Glaring Fleshraker to disrupt the opponent's hand and board state, allowing for a strong control game plan.

The deck focuses on using artifacts to quickly build a board presence and overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures and equipment. By utilizing cards like Urza's Saga and Mox Opal, the deck can quickly ramp up mana and deploy threats early in the game. With a combination of efficient creatures like Kappa Cannoneer and Pinnacle Emissary, along with versatile artifacts like Claws of Gix and Weapons Manufacturing, the deck aims to put pressure on the opponent and close out the game quickly. Additionally, cards like Salvage Titan and Experimental Synthesizer provide additional value and resilience to help maintain a strong board presence.

The main concept of this deck is to utilize a combination of discard spells, counterspells, and card draw to disrupt the opponent's game plan while setting up a combo win condition involving Thassa's Oracle or Jace, Wielder of Mysteries. By efficiently controlling the game through disruption and drawing cards, the deck aims to assemble the required pieces to win without relying on traditional lands for mana. The deck also includes cards like Abandon Attachments and Hydroelectric Specimen for additional utility and interaction with the opponent.
